claims.statement: Effective temperature is a surface luminosity-radius proxy and does not directly determine stellar core temperature.

sources.note: Reference for the Stefan-Boltzmann effective temperature relation.

claims.statement: Fusion-zone estimates require stellar-structure context rather than spectral class alone.

validityDomain.system: reduced-order stellar structure and profile import
constraints: reduced-order proxy unless a full profile is imported
MESA-backed profiles are preferred for real stellar evolution work

sources.citation: Paxton, B. et al. (2011). Modules for Experiments in Stellar Astrophysics (MESA). Astrophysical Journal Supplement Series 192, 3.
sources.note: MESA solves coupled stellar structure and composition equations with configurable physics modules.

claims.statement: The pp chain is the dominant hydrogen-burning channel for Sun-like and lower-mass main-sequence stars in this Stage 1 prior.

sources.citation: Adelberger, E. G. et al. (2011). Solar fusion cross sections II: the pp chain and CNO cycles. Reviews of Modern Physics.
sources.note: Solar fusion cross-section source for pp-chain and CNO-cycle microphysics.

claims.statement: The CNO cycle may dominate hydrogen burning in hotter, higher-mass main-sequence stars in this Stage 1 prior.

sources.citation: Adelberger, E. G. et al. (2011). Solar fusion cross sections II: the pp chain and CNO cycles. Reviews of Modern Physics.
sources.note: Solar fusion cross-section source for pp-chain and CNO-cycle microphysics.

claims.statement: Fusion-zone volume is represented by cumulative nuclear luminosity radii such as r10, r50, and r90 rather than by the whole stellar volume.

constraints: Stage 1 uses reduced-order or imported profile metadata

sources.citation: Paxton, B. et al. (2011). Modules for Experiments in Stellar Astrophysics (MESA). Astrophysical Journal Supplement Series 192, 3.
sources.note: MESA profile context for stellar energy-generation and structure modeling.

claims.statement: Spectral recovery of h is calibration/inference bookkeeping only because the SI value of the Planck constant is exact.

constraints: does not measure a varying h
does not infer a new fundamental constant value

sources.note: NIST reference for the exact SI value of the Planck constant.

claims.statement: Cool-star spectral inference needs stellar-atmosphere model support when molecular opacity and bands dominate the spectrum.

constraints: atmosphere model support is required for higher-confidence spectral inference

sources.citation: Husser, T. O. et al. (2013). A new extensive library of PHOENIX stellar atmospheres and synthetic spectra. Astronomy & Astrophysics.
sources.note: Synthetic stellar atmosphere framework used as a guardrail against blackbody-only overinterpretation.

claims.statement: A StarMap fusion graph can provide an astrometric and population-structure prior, not direct quantum-gravity evidence.

sources.citation: Gaia Collaboration (2023). Gaia Data Release 3: Summary of the content and survey properties. Astronomy & Astrophysics.
sources.note: Gaia DR3 provides positions, parallax, proper motions, photometry, and radial velocities for large stellar samples.

claims.statement: Neutron-star glitch context is a compact-object quantum-fluid proxy and is not PP-chain fusion.

sources.citation: Khomenko, V. et al. (2020). Turbulent, pinned superfluids in neutron stars and pulsar glitch recoveries. Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society.
sources.note: Glitch context for pinned superfluid/vortex and crust-core coupling models.

claims.statement: Stellar fusion microphysics may inform astrophysical QST priors but cannot directly support ER=EPR, wormhole density, spacetime metric equivalence, propulsion, or stress-energy claims.

sources.citation: Maldacena, J. and Susskind, L. (2013). Cool horizons for entangled black holes. arXiv:1306.0533.
sources.equation: ER=EPR boundary context only; no stellar-fusion equation or inventory relation is asserted.
sources.note: ER=EPR context is not a direct stellar-fusion inference.

sources.citation: Carcassi, G., Landini, G. and Aidala, C. (2024). Classical mechanics as the high-entropy limit of quantum mechanics. arXiv:2411.00972.
sources.note: Entropy visibility context for QST-style priors.
